# Intercom's AI Agent Fin: Resolution Rates, Pricing, and Honest Assessment (2026)

## Summary

Published 2026-03-03. One of the most detailed independent assessments of Fin's real-world performance, with production metrics and cost modeling. The author provides a frank cost analysis and break-even calculation that is directly usable in the stinger's decision guide.

**Resolution rates (production data from Intercom, 7,000+ customers):**

- Average resolution rate: 67%, improving roughly 1% per month
- Simple, well-documented products: 80-93% resolution rates
- Complex B2B products: 50-65% resolution rates
- Early implementations (thin documentation): 40-50% resolution rates

**Pricing (as of early 2026):**

| Plan | Cost | Notable features |
|---|---|---|
| Essential | $29/seat/month | Shared inbox, basic automation, Fin AI |
| Advanced | $85/seat/month | Workflows, multiple inboxes, advanced automation, SLA management, CSAT |
| Expert | $132/seat/month | Workload management, custom roles |

Note: A separate source (StackCompare, March 2026) indicates Fin AI is only available on Advanced and Expert plans, not Essential -- this contradicts the above table. The stinger should flag this discrepancy and recommend verifying directly at intercom.com/pricing before advising.

**Fin AI pricing (usage-based):** $0.99 per outcome/resolution.

**Cost model (illustrative):**

| Monthly volume | Fin cost |
|---|---|
| 500 resolutions | $495 |
| 2,000 resolutions | $1,980 |
| 5,000 resolutions | $4,950 |
| 10,000 resolutions | $9,900 |

Break-even calculation: A junior support agent (UK) costs ~$2,500-3,000/mo fully loaded. Fin at 3,000+ resolutions/mo exceeds that cost -- but provides 24/7 coverage and volume-spike resilience that humans cannot match at the same cost.

**Key risk -- pricing unpredictability:** Per-resolution pricing means a viral launch or service outage can triple AI costs overnight. The author recommends setting a monthly Fin budget cap with hard limits.

**Fin's hard dependency:** Fin is only as good as the knowledge base it reads. "Companies that deploy Fin without investing in their help content are paying $0.99 per disappointment." Recommended: do not deploy Fin until you have substantive, accurate documentation for at least the top 20 most common questions.

## Key takeaways

- 67% average resolution rate across 7,000+ Intercom customers -- the highest published benchmark in the support AI category
- Cost predictability is the main risk: per-resolution pricing can spike unexpectedly; set hard monthly limits
- Knowledge base quality is the #1 lever for Fin performance -- thin docs = low resolution rates
- Fin is not appropriate for complex B2B products with high edge-case volume until documentation is mature
- The stinger should recommend starting with Intercom Essential ($29/seat) before committing to Advanced ($85/seat)
